title: Orontium asarina
taxonomy: Orontium asarina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Pyrolaceae. This genus is further grouped into the order Ericales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is also known as the golden club, a name that refers to its distinctive yellow club-shaped spathes. The classification of Orontium asarina is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Magnoliophyta, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Ericales, Family: Pyrolaceae, Genus: Orontium, Species: O. australis. Orontium asarina is native to the southeastern United States.
